sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Stephanie Morales, a 36-year-old female, presents with a 2-day history of ankle pain, which started after slipping on ice and twisting her left ankle.
She reports falling and experiencing severe pain, swelling, and difficulty walking.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation, which has provided some relief.
The pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, with no pain radiating to her calf, lower leg, or knee.
She denies bleeding or significant swelling.
Past medical history: Stephanie has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She is taking metformin and norvasc, with a hemoglobin A1c of 6.8.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Metformin [dose and frequency NOT MENTIONED]
Norvasc [dose and frequency NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70, pulse rate NOT MENTIONED, oxygen saturation NOT MENTIONED, temperature NOT MENTIONED.
Physical exam: The patient has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with some swelling and redness.
The ankle exam shows pain with valgus stressing.
The patient has normal sensation and pulses in the affected area.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I suspect the patient has an ankle sprain, but I would like to order an x-ray to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; Diabetes; High blood pressure.
Plan:
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I will give the patient a splint and crutches to avoid weightbearing until the x-ray results are available.
I will order an x-ray of the ankle.
I will also order baseline labs, including a hemoglobin A1c, to monitor the patient's diabetes.
I will refill the patient's norvasc prescription.
Follow-up: I will contact the patient once the x-ray results are available to determine the next course of action.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer the patient to orthopedics.
